quote: Bananas are curved because they grow towards the sun against gravity. This process is called negative geotropism.
author: Did you know?

quote: Wombat feces are cube-shaped, which stops them from rolling away so they can mark their territory.
author: Did you know?

quote: Honey never spoils. You can theoretically eat 3,000-year-old Egyptian tomb honey and it is perfectly safe.
author: Did you know?

quote: A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus. It takes Venus longer to rotate once than to complete one orbit around the Sun.
author: Did you know?

quote: Clouds can weigh over a million pounds. A typical cumulus cloud has a mass equivalent to about 100 adult elephants.
author: Did you know?

quote: Octopuses have three hearts, nine brains, and blue blood. Two hearts pump blood to the gills, while one pumps it to the body.
author: Did you know?

quote: Sloths can hold their breath longer than dolphins can. By slowing their heart rate down, they can hold it for 40 minutes.
author: Did you know?

quote: A single strand of spaghetti is officially called a "spaghetto".
author: Did you know?

quote: There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way galaxy. Earth has 3 trillion trees; the galaxy has 400 billion stars.
author: Did you know?

quote: Cleopatra lived closer to the moon landing than to the construction of the Great Pyramid of Giza.
author: Did you know?

quote: Chewing gum while peeling onions will prevent you from crying by dispersing the irritant gas before it reaches your eyes.
author: Did you know?

quote: The inventor of the Pringles can was buried inside one. When Fredric Baur died in 2008, his ashes were placed in a Pringles can.
author: Did you know?

quote: Sharks existed before trees. Sharks have been on Earth for 400 million years, while trees only appeared 350 million years ago.
author: Did you know?

quote: A bolt of lightning is five times hotter than the surface of the sun.
author: Did you know?

quote: A blue whale's tongue can weigh as much as an entire adult elephant.
author: Did you know?

quote: Your brain uses about 20 percent of your body's total oxygen and energy, despite representing only 2 percent of your body weight.
author: Did you know?

quote: The tongue is the only muscle in the human body that is attached at only one end.
author: Did you know?

quote: Strawberries are not actual berries, but bananas, pumpkins, and watermelons are.
author: Did you know?

quote: Human teeth are just as strong as shark teeth.
author: Did you know?

quote: Pineapples take almost three years to grow and mature to harvest.
author: Did you know?

quote: Cow's milk can vary in taste depending on the grass the cow eats.
author: Did you know?

quote: The first commercial passenger flight lasted only 23 minutes.
author: Did you know?

quote: The heart of a shrimp is located in its head.
author: Did you know?

quote: A snail can sleep for up to three years without eating.
author: Did you know?

quote: Slugs have four noses, which help them locate food.
author: Did you know?

quote: Koalas sleep up to 22 hours a day to digest the tough eucalyptus leaves.
author: Did you know?

quote: The Eiffel Tower can be 15 centimeters taller during the summer due to thermal expansion.
author: Did you know?

quote: Some turtles can breathe through their butts to survive cold winters underwater.
author: Did you know?

quote: Polar bears have black skin under their white fur to absorb warmth from the sun.
author: Did you know?

quote: The wood frog can freeze solid during winter and thaw out alive in spring.
author: Did you know?

quote: Groupers can change their sex from female to male later in life.
author: Did you know?

quote: The average person spends about 6 months of their life waiting for red lights to turn green.
author: Did you know?

quote: There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than there are atoms in the observable universe.
author: Did you know?

quote: Cows have best friends and get stressed when they are separated from them.
author: Did you know?

quote: Sea otters hold hands while sleeping so they do not drift apart.
author: Did you know?

quote: Flamingos are pink because of the carotenoid pigments in the shrimp they eat.
author: Did you know?

quote: A group of crows is called a "murder".
author: Did you know?

quote: A group of owls is called a "parliament".
author: Did you know?

quote: A group of flamingos is called a "flamboyance".
author: Did you know?

quote: The average cloud has the same water content as 100 large pools.
author: Did you know?

quote: Apples float in water because 25 percent of their volume is actually air.
author: Did you know?

quote: A teaspoon of a neutron star would weigh about 6 billion tons.
author: Did you know?

quote: Standard Lego bricks made in 1958 still fit perfectly with bricks made today.
author: Did you know?

quote: The dry valleys of Antarctica are the driest places on Earth, with no rain for 2 million years.
author: Did you know?

quote: Hot water freezes faster than cold water (known as the Mpemba effect).
author: Did you know?

quote: The footprint left by astronauts on the moon will last for millions of years because there is no wind.
author: Did you know?

quote: Human bones are about four times stronger than concrete of equal weight.
author: Did you know?

quote: The human eye can distinguish about 10 million different colors.
author: Did you know?

quote: An adult human is made up of roughly 7 octillion atoms.
author: Did you know?

quote: Your fingernails grow about three times faster than your toenails.
author: Did you know?

quote: The electric eel can produce a shock of up to 860 volts to deter predators.
author: Did you know?

quote: Saturn's rings are not solid; they are made of billions of ice and rock particles.
author: Did you know?

quote: The first computer mouse was carved out of a single block of wood by Douglas Engelbart.
author: Did you know?

quote: The smell of freshly cut grass is actually a distress signal plants release.
author: Did you know?

quote: The original name for search engine Google was "Backrub".
author: Did you know?

quote: The dot over the lowercase letter "i" or "j" is officially called a "tittle".
author: Did you know?

quote: In Switzerland, it is illegal to own just one guinea pig because they get lonely.
author: Did you know?

quote: The first toy ever advertised on television was Mr. Potato Head in 1952.
author: Did you know?

quote: A giant squid's eyes are as large as standard dinner plates.
author: Did you know?

quote: Jupiter is so massive that all other planets in the solar system could fit inside it.
author: Did you know?

quote: Your nose and ears never stop growing throughout your entire life.
author: Did you know?

quote: You shed about 40 pounds of skin cells over the course of your life.
author: Did you know?

quote: The human body contains enough fat to make seven bars of soap.
author: Did you know?

quote: An octopus has to use its brain to manually tell its arms how to move.
author: Did you know?

quote: Jellyfish are 95 percent water and have absolutely no brain, heart, or bones.
author: Did you know?

quote: Lobsters do not age biologically and can theoretically live forever unless caught or infected.
author: Did you know?

quote: Cats have 32 muscles in each of their ears, allowing them to rotate them 180 degrees.
author: Did you know?

quote: The water you drink today is the exact same water dinosaurs drank millions of years ago.
author: Did you know?

quote: Earth is the only known planet in our solar system that is not named after a mythological god.
author: Did you know?

quote: Wind is completely silent until it blows against a physical object.
author: Did you know?

quote: Sound travels about four times faster in water than it does in air.
author: Did you know?

quote: A day on Jupiter lasts only 10 hours, the shortest day in the solar system.
author: Did you know?

quote: Raindrops are not shaped like tears; they actually look like tiny hamburger buns.
author: Did you know?

quote: The hummingbird is the only bird in the world that can fly backward.
author: Did you know?

quote: An adult elephant can consume up to 300 pounds of food in a single day.
author: Did you know?

quote: Ants do not have lungs; they breathe through tiny holes all over their bodies.
author: Did you know?

quote: The total weight of all the ants on Earth is roughly equal to the total weight of all humans.
author: Did you know?

quote: Owls do not have eyeballs; they have long eye tubes that are completely stationary.
author: Did you know?

quote: The fear of long words is ironically called "Hippopotomonstrosesquippedaliophobia".
author: Did you know?

quote: Reindeer eyes turn gold in the summer and deep blue in the winter to help them see in the dark.
author: Did you know?

quote: Standard copy paper is made of fibers that can be recycled up to seven times before breaking down.
author: Did you know?

quote: The term "astronaut" comes from Greek words meaning "star sailor".
author: Did you know?

quote: Outer space is not a complete vacuum; it contains about 5 atoms per cubic meter.
author: Did you know?

quote: Sound cannot travel in space because there are no air molecules to carry the sound waves.
author: Did you know?

quote: The sunset on Mars appears blue to the human eye due to dust particles.
author: Did you know?

quote: A lightning bolt can carry up to 1 billion volts of electricity.
author: Did you know?

quote: The brain itself cannot feel pain because it does not have pain receptors.
author: Did you know?

quote: An average person produces enough saliva in their lifetime to fill two swimming pools.
author: Did you know?

quote: The most common color for highlighters is yellow because it does not leave a shadow when photocopied.
author: Did you know?

quote: A flock of ravens is officially called a "unkindness".
author: Did you know?

quote: A group of zebras is officially called a "dazzle".
author: Did you know?

quote: Sea otters have a built-in pocket under their forelegs to store their favorite rocks.
author: Did you know?

quote: The first alarm clock could only ring at one exact time: 4:00 AM.
author: Did you know?

quote: Matchsticks were invented after the lighter was already in use.
author: Did you know?

quote: Honeybees can recognize human faces and remember them for their entire lives.
author: Did you know?

quote: The average human body contains enough iron to forge a three-inch nail.
author: Did you know?

quote: Cows do not have upper front teeth; they use their hard palate to crush grass.
author: Did you know?

quote: Some bamboo species can grow up to 35 inches in a single 24-hour period.
author: Did you know?

quote: The small pocket in your blue jeans was originally designed to hold a pocket watch.
author: Did you know?

quote: Bananas are naturally radioactive because they contain high levels of potassium.
author: Did you know?
